topic.name: Tax Incentives and Education
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at providing tax deductions and credits to promote education and manage debt relief related to financial obligations.

Document 1: allow an unlimited tax deduction for qualified tuition and related expenses
replace the Hope Scholarship tax credit with an earned tuition tax credit
Document 2: extend through 2013 the exclusion from gross income of income attributable to the discharge of indebtedness on a principal residence
Document 3: allow an exclusion from gross income for income attributable to the discharge of indebtedness relating to securities

topic.name: Medicare and Medicaid Innovations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative initiatives aimed at improving care management, access to services, and informed decision-making for Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries.

Document 1: establish the REDUCE demonstration program
reduce hospital admissions and the use of emergency health care services
Document 2: establish a demonstration project under title XVIII (Medicare)
evaluate the benefits of providing payment for items and services needed for the administration, within the homes of Medicare beneficiaries
Document 3: amends title XVIII (Medicare) part C
require the information the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) provides to promote informed choice on available part C coverage options

topic.name: Education Health and Safety Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving health and safety standards in educational institutions.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act to allow the Secretary of Health and Human Services to make demonstration grants to states
to reduce the student-to-school nurse ratio in public secondary schools, elementary schools, and kindergarten.
Document 2: Directs the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency to review, update, revise, and publish the Radon Measurements In Schools Guidelines
to conduct short-term tests of radon levels in their public elementary and secondary schools.

topic.name: Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: The topic focuses on amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States regarding the temporary suspension of duties on specific goods and manufacturing equipment.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on certain manufacturing equipment.
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on Ethene, tetrafluoro-oxidized, polymerized, reduced, methyl esters, reduced.
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend temporarily the suspension of duty on Reactive Red 198.

topic.name: Veterans' Health and Training Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving training for veterans' services and addressing mental health issues among veterans.

Document 1: Mandatory Veteran Specialist Training Act of 2009
reduces training period for veterans' outreach specialists
Document 2: Heroes at Home Act of 2007
establishes training for family caregivers of veterans
Document 3: COMBAT PTSD Act
defines service-connection for veterans' disability compensation related to PTSD

topic.name: Offshore Energy Regulation
topic.description: Legislative measures and regulations governing offshore oil and gas leasing, production, and environmental protections.

Document 1: Allows the governor of a state... to petition the Secretary of the Interior to make a new producing area of the state eligible for: (1) oil leasing, gas leasing, or both...
Requires the Secretary of the Treasury to deposit 45% of all rentals, royalties, bonus bids...
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of the Interior to issue regulations that require each Bureau of Ocean Energy Management... employee to be subject to random testing...
Prohibits the Secretary from exempting any category of activity of a vessel or facility engaged in OCS oil or gas drilling...
Document 3: Establishes in the Treasury the American-Made Energy Trust Fund for alternative and renewable energy incentives...
Directs the Secretary of the Interior to establish, implement, and administer a competitive oil and gas leasing program on the Coastal Plain of Alaska.

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Policy
topic.description: Legislation focused on the regulation and management of immigration processes and refugee admissions in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act (INA) to revise provisions regarding the detention and removal of aliens ordered removed.
Extends the removal (and detention) period beyond 90 days if: the alien fails or refuses to comply with the removal order.
Document 2: Amends the Departments of Commerce, Justice, and State, the Judiciary, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 1993 to make the EB-5 visa (alien investor) regional center program permanent.
Document 3: Prohibits the U.S. refugee admission of an alien who is a national of, has habitually resided in, or is claiming refugee status due to events in any country containing terrorist-controlled territory.

topic.name: EMP Weapons Accountability
topic.description: Legislation focused on the oversight of foreign electromagnetic pulse weapons programs.

Document 1: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.
Document 2: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.

topic.name: Federal Land Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, conveyance, and restoration of federal lands in the United States.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to convey to the city of Fruit Heights, Utah, all interest of the United States in approximately 100 acres of identified National Forest System land.
Requires the city to use such land only for public purposes.
Document 2: Amends the Healthy Forests Restoration Act of 2003 to direct the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) and the Secretary of the Interior, as appropriate, to implement procedures to ensure that not less than 600,000 acres of federal land each fiscal year are treated with mechanical treatments intended to produce merchantable wood.
Directs the Secretary to initiate salvage activities on federal land affected by a catastrophic event.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to convey, to the local communities in South Dakota that are currently managing specified community cemeteries, all interest of the United States in the parcels of National Forest System land that contain the cemeteries.

topic.name: Child Welfare and Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at enhancing the welfare and protection of children, particularly in relation to immigration enforcement, child trafficking, and exploitation.

Document 1: Humane Enforcement and Legal Protections for Separated Children Act

require that state plans for foster care and adoption assistance include provisions regarding foster care children with a parent, legal guardian, or primary caregiver relative who is in immigration detainment
Document 2: amends the Child Abuse Prevention and Treatment Act to require a state plan for its child protective services system
training child protective services workers about identifying, assessing, and providing comprehensive services for children who are sex trafficking victims
Document 3: Amends the federal criminal code to add child sexual exploitation and child pornography to the crimes
interception of wire, oral, or electronic communications may be authorized in state investigations

topic.name: Autism Spectrum Disorder Legislation
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at improving services and support for individuals with autism spectrum disorders and related developmental disabilities.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act to extend and reauthorize appropriations for programs established under the Combating Autism Act of 2006
Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to submit a progress report on activities related to autism spectrum disorders.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish a demonstration program to award grants for autism navigator programs
Requires the HHS Secretary to develop a standard curriculum for training first responders in assisting individuals with autism.
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to award grants for services to children and adults with mental illnesses, including those with autism
Provides for reports to Congress on the needs of mental health and substance abuse providers.

topic.name: Pharmaceutical Regulation and Safety
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving the regulation, safety, and accessibility of pharmaceuticals and drug-related data.

Document 1: Safer Drug Assessment Technology Advancement Act or the Safer DATA Act - Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish minimum standards for collection and transmission of postmarketing data elements.
Requires the Secretary to improve the transparency of pharmaceutical data and allow patients and health care providers better access to pharmaceutical data.
Document 2: Patient Health and Real Medication Access Cost Savings Act of 2009 or the PHARMACY Bill - Declares that a consumer shall have the right to choose to purchase prescription drugs from any domestic pharmacy.
Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to issue regulations that prohibit a pharmacy benefit manager (PBM) from providing incentives to...

topic.name: Water Recycling and Infrastructure Projects
topic.description: Legislative initiatives aimed at enhancing water recycling facilities and related infrastructure in various regions of the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior...
to enhance and restore riparian habitat in the Black Wash Sonoran Desert ecosystem
Document 2: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior...
to participate in the design, planning, and construction of an advanced water treatment plant facility and recycled water system
Document 3: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior...
to enhance and restore riparian habitat in the Black Wash Sonoran Desert ecosystem

topic.name: Mortgage Regulation and Support
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating government-sponsored enterprises and providing support for homeowners facing mortgage challenges.

Document 1: Appoints the Federal Housing Finance Agency (FHFA) receiver of the Federal National Mortgage Association (Fannie Mae) and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (Freddie Mac)
Requires the FHFA as receiver to manage the combined assets of the GSEs to obtain resolutions that maximize the return for the taxpayer.
Document 2: Amends the Federal National Mortgage Association Charter Act to declare that stock, obligations, securities, participations, or other instruments issued by the Federal National Mortgage Association (Fannie Mae) shall not be exempt securities

States that transactions involving the initial disposition by an approved seller of pooled certificates acquired by the seller from Fannie Mae or Freddie Mac shall be deemed to be transactions by a person other than as an issuer, underwriter, or dealer.
Document 3: Amends the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act of 2008 (EESA) to authorize the Secretary of the Treasury to use otherwise unobligated amounts under the Troubled Asset Relief Program (TARP)
to enable nonprofit counseling intermediaries and nonprofit legal organizations to provide legal assistance to homeowners of owner-occupied homes.

topic.name: Coral Reef Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at the conservation, restoration, and management of coral reef ecosystems in the United States.

Document 1: amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000
establish the Center of Excellence in Coral Reef Ecosystem Science Cooperative Institute.
Document 2: amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000
make grants for projects for the conservation of coral reef ecosystems.
Document 3: extends the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds
authorize actions to minimize injury to a coral reef or loss of an ecosystem function.

topic.name: Agricultural Policy and Education
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to agricultural practices, crop insurance, food security, and nutrition education.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Crop Insurance Act to reduce crop insurance assistance for crops grown on native sod acreage.
Noninsured crop disaster assistance for first four years.
Document 2: Revises agricultural and related programs and provisions respecting producer income, conservation, and trade.
Includes aspects of nutrition and rural development.
Document 3: Amends the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act to require nutrition education in schools.
Requires instruction to meet specified nutritional standards.

topic.name: Clean Energy Initiatives
topic.description: Legislation and programs promoting the development and implementation of clean energy technologies and efficiency standards.

Document 1: establish a standard that requires electric utilities to obtain an increasing percentage of their base quantity of electricity from clean energy or energy efficiency
Requires the Secretary of Energy (DOE) to establish a clean energy credit trading program
Document 2: promoting domestic development, manufacture, and deployment of clean technologies
enhance U.S. economic, environmental, and energy security by promoting domestic development
Document 3: direct the Secretary of Energy to make loans as well as grants for qualified electric transportation projects
develop systems to enable plug-in electric drive vehicles to enhance the availability of emergency back-up power

topic.name: Data Breach Notification Laws
topic.description: Legislation requiring timely notification and protective measures in the event of data breaches involving personal information.

Document 1: establish policies, procedures, and standards for agencies to follow in the event of a breach of data security
timely notification to individuals whose sensitive personal information could be compromised
Document 2: notify an individual within two business days after discovery of any breach of security
resulted in the theft of or unlawful access to the individual's personally identifiable information
Document 3: requires covered entities to take reasonable measures to protect and secure data
give notice of any breach of security that the entity reasonably believes has caused or will cause identity theft

topic.name: Identity Theft and Tax Fraud Prevention
topic.description: Legislation aimed at preventing identity theft and tax fraud through enhanced IRS regulations and penalties.

Document 1: establish requirements and authorities for the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) related to: (1) preventing identity theft and tax refund fraud
increases civil and criminal penalties associated with identity theft
Document 2: establish a plan to reduce the administrative time required to process and resolve cases of tax-related identity theft
implement a program to prevent the processing of a tax return by an identity thief
Document 3: impose a fine and/or prison term on any person who knowingly or willfully misappropriates another person's tax identification number
require the Commissioner of Internal Revenue to submit to the Senate Committee on Finance an annual report on the number of reported cases of tax fraud

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Compliance
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at regulating financial institutions and their activities to ensure stability and protect investors.

Document 1: Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA) to declare that no person shall be considered a fiduciary to an employee benefit plan
Prescribes criteria for considering a representative of a Special Entity to be independent of a swap dealer.
Document 2: Amends the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956 to prohibit a bank holding company from holding more than 10% of the total amount of deposits
Prescribes minimum leverage ratios (6% of average total consolidated assets) and balance sheet leverage ratios.
Document 3: Amends the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act with respect to the prohibition against certain federal assistance to swaps entities
Requires any covered depository institution exempted from the prohibition to limit its swap and security-based swap activities to hedging and similar risk mitigating activities.

topic.name: Recreational Access and Conservation
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing recreational fishing, hunting, and land access while ensuring conservation of wildlife and habitats.

Document 1: Requires federal public land management officials to facilitate the use of federal public lands for fishing, sport hunting, and recreational shooting.
Prohibits actions concerning recreational opportunities from being considered major federal actions affecting the environment.
Document 2: Requires federal land management agencies to provide public reports on lands available for hunting and fishing.
Mandates development of plans to ensure access to recreational lands.
Document 3: Amends the Endangered Species Act to expedite consultation on impacts to endangered species during flood control actions.
Directs relocation of threatened or endangered species affected by agency actions.

topic.name: Postal Designations and Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures designating postal facilities and adjustments to benefits for specific government employees.

Document 1: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 101 East Market Street in Long Beach, California, as the 'Juanita Millender-McDonald Post Office.'
Document 2: This bill provides that calculation of the basic annuity benefits for an air traffic controller with at least five years of service...
The Office of Personnel Management shall establish such procedures as necessary to provide for notification to each annuitant affected by this bill.
Document 3: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 3031 Veterans Road West in Staten Island, New York, as the 'Leonard Montalto Post Office Building.'

topic.name: Rail Safety and Security Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving safety, security, and infrastructure for rail transportation in the United States.

Document 3: Directs the Under Secretary for Border and Transportation Security to complete a vulnerability assessment of freight and passenger rail transportation.
Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to award grants directly to public transportation agencies for allowable capital and operational security improvements.

topic.name: Nuclear Science and Education Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and initiatives aimed at enhancing nuclear science education, workforce development, and the management of nuclear materials.

Document 1: awarding grants to undergraduate institutions for nuclear science scholarships
recommendations for developing a robust nuclear workforce
Document 2: establishing procedures for spent nuclear fuel storage
moving spent nuclear fuel to certified dry casks
Document 3: appropriations for research programs to stimulate competitiveness
co-funding projects in nuclear science and related fields

topic.name: Financial Savings and Investment Policies
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing savings and investment opportunities for individuals, particularly in relation to retirement and education funding.

1: Establishes an ASPIRE Fund in the Treasury, including a Lifetime Savings Account.
Creates the ASPIRE Fund Board to establish a default investment program.
2: Repeals provisions of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act that limited payments for medications from health savings accounts.
Rescinds appropriated but unobligated discretionary funds determined by the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB).
3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to suspend in 2008 and 2009 requirements for minimum distributions from tax-deferred retirement plans.
Permits taxpayers who have already received a minimum distribution to recontribute such distribution to their retirement plans.

topic.name: Small Business Innovation and Support
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing support and funding for small businesses through innovation programs and technology transfer initiatives.

Document 1: reauthorize and make permanent the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) and Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) programs
enhanced small business access to federal innovation investments
Document 2: Directs the Under Secretary of Technology of the Department of Commerce to award grants to eligible entities
technology transfer, development, and utilization programs for businesses

Document 3: authorize the Secretary of the Army to accept and expend funds contributed by a private entity
private entity includes a business association, a cooperative of businesses, associations, or individuals

topic.name: Federal Recognition and Support for Indian Tribes
topic.description: Legislation aimed at providing federal recognition, support, and consultation processes for Indian tribes in the United States.

Document 1: loan forgiveness to borrowers who are members of an Indian tribe
employed as a full-time teacher for five consecutive complete school years in an Indian school
Document 2: extends federal recognition to the Chickahominy Tribe
eligible for all federal services and benefits provided to federally recognized Indian tribes
Document 3: consultation with Indian tribes involves more than simply notifying a tribe
ensure meaningful and timely input by Indian tribes before undertaking any activity that may have substantial direct impacts

topic.name: Social Security Act Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes and provisions related to the Social Security Act, focusing on health care programs and benefits for individuals.

Document 1: Amends title XI of the Social Security Act
study, and report to Congress on, the effect of applying the anti-kickback laws
Document 2: amends title II (Old Age, Survivors and Disability Insurance) of the Social Security Act
prohibit the recovery of overpayments through tax refund offsets
Document 3: Amends title II (Old Age, Survivors and Disability Insurance) of the Social Security Act
revise the process for congressional consideration of international social security agreements

topic.name: Regulatory Oversight and Reform
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating the creation, modification, and review of federal rules and regulations, particularly in relation to their economic impact and environmental standards.

Document 1: Prohibits any federal department or agency from publishing new rules or regulations
until January 31, 2013.
Document 2: Prohibits the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) from lowering its existing national ambient air quality standards
Requires the EPA, in promulgating a primary or secondary NAAQS for ozone, to only consider a county to be a nonattainment area on the basis of direct air quality monitoring.
Document 3: Requires each federal agency to establish a plan for the periodic review
each covered rule shall cease to have effect seven years after the final version of such rule is published unless renewed by the agency.

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Policy and Sanctions
topic.description: The documents discuss U.S. legislative measures related to foreign policy, specifically focusing on sanctions against Iran and support for Ukraine.

Document 1: Congress favors the Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action relating to Iran's nuclear program
Document 2: Support for the Sovereignty, Integrity, Democracy, and Economic Stability of Ukraine Act of 2014
Directs the Secretary of State to assist the government of Ukraine to recover assets linked to acts of corruption
Document 3: This bill prohibits the President from issuing a license that permits a person to conduct an offshore U.S. dollar clearing system for transactions involving the government of Iran

topic.name: Housing Assistance Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at improving housing assistance and protections for vulnerable populations, particularly low-income families and homeless individuals.

Document 1: Amends the McKinney-Vento Homeless Assistance Act to redefine 'homeless'...
Requires the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to aggregate and publicly report information...
Document 2: Amends the United States Housing Act of 1937 to require a public housing agency to contract to make section 8 rental assistance payments directly to a low-income family...
Document 3: Amends the United States Housing Act of 1937 to revise requirements for public housing agency inspections...
Directs the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to establish standards and procedures for assessing PHA performance...

topic.name: Election Campaign Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the regulation of election campaign activities, including fundraising and disclosure requirements.

Document 1: prohibit an individual holding federal office from soliciting funds directly
participate in a fundraising event, including planning or attending it
Document 2: eliminates the mandatory printing of bills and resolutions
available for the use of Congress only in an electronic format
Document 3: require all election-related designations, statements, and reports
filed directly with the Federal Election Commission

topic.name: Consumer Financial Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting consumers in financial transactions, particularly regarding credit and overdraft practices.

Document 1: prohibit a depository institution from engaging in unfair or deceptive acts or practices in connection with overdraft coverage
requires clear disclosure of overdraft coverage fees
Document 2: prohibit a credit card issuer from changing the terms of a credit card under an open end consumer credit plan
establishes a cardholder right to repay all existing balances on a terminated or expired credit card account
Document 3: allows a person to request the removal of a previously reported default regarding a qualified education loan
requires the Government Accountability Office to report on any hurdles borrowers experience with the private loan rehabilitation program

topic.name: Communication Policy and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and regulatory actions related to communication services, particularly focusing on television transitions and access for vulnerable populations.

Document 1: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to require the FCC to create a comprehensive nationwide public education campaign
Provides for grants for public education outreach and installation assistance to older individuals and other vulnerable populations.
Document 2: Directs the FCC to authorize owners and operators of wireless microphones to have access to the geolocation database
Specifies registration sites for the purpose of identifying locations to be protected through database registration.
Document 3: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to require the FCC to seek and consider petitions requesting the expansion of a satellite or cable license
Permits secondary transmissions to an adjacent in-state market area.

topic.name: Veterans' Benefits and Compensation
topic.description: This topic encompasses the various benefits and compensation programs available to the families of military personnel, including death gratuities and dependency compensation for surviving spouses.

Document 1: death gratuity for members of the Armed Forces who die while serving
payable to parent, brother, or sister as designated by the member
Document 2: increases the monthly rates of veterans' dependency and indemnity compensation
prohibits benefits from being reduced or offset due to eligibility for dependency and indemnity compensation
Document 3: entitles the spouse or unremarried widow or widower of a veteran to receive additional preference for competitive service

topic.name: Water Quality Regulation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at ensuring safe drinking water by regulating contaminants and assisting water systems in compliance.

Document 1: Amends the Safe Drinking Water Act to direct the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to publish a health advisory for perchlorate
require consumer confidence reports by community water systems to disclose the presence of perchlorate in drinking water
Document 2: Amends the Safe Drinking Water Act to authorize appropriations to the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) for technical assistance
Requires the Administrator, in providing grants for such assistance, to give priority to qualified small systems organizations
Document 3: Amends the Toxic Substances Control Act (TSCA) to exclude from the definition of 'abatement' any renovation, remodeling, landscaping
Requires the Administrator to conduct a lead-based paint certification study prior to proposing any new regulation applicable to target housing

topic.name: Labor Rights and Employment Programs
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at protecting labor rights, facilitating employment opportunities, and supporting specific groups such as veterans and federal employees.

Document 1: Reauthorizes the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) homeless veterans reintegration programs
Directs the Secretary of Labor to make grants to programs and facilities that provide dedicated services for homeless women veterans and homeless veterans with children.
Document 2: Amends the National Labor Relations Act (NLRA) to make it an unlawful labor practice for a labor organization or its agents to interfere with the rights of employees to organize
Adds a requirement that representatives be selected by secret ballot in an election conducted by the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB).
Document 3: Excludes employees of the Internal Revenue Service from provisions of federal personnel law allowing federal employees to organize
This bill aims to prevent unionization of Revenue Service Employees.

topic.name: U.S. Legal Accountability and Immigration Policies
topic.description: This cluster addresses U.S. legislation related to legal accountability for foreign states and immigration enforcement practices, particularly concerning the treatment of children and detainees.

Document 1: advocacy within the governing bodies of international organizations for the full compensation and fair treatment of persons in whose favor judgments have been awarded by U.S. courts
protection of economic interests of persons and nations that benefit from a reliable flow of foreign capital
Document 2: require DHS detention facilities to implement procedures to ensure that child custody and family interests can be considered in any immigration detention action
develop memoranda of understanding with child welfare agencies and community organizations that protect the best interests of children of detained individuals
Document 3: prohibitions on the use of funds available to any U.S. agency to construct or modify any facility in the United States to house individuals detained in Guantanamo Bay
authorize the Secretary of Defense to temporarily transfer Guantanamo detainees to DOD medical facilities in the United States for medical treatment

topic.name: Education Policy Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at reforming and regulating various aspects of education, including funding, student privacy, and legal protections for students.

Document 1: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965
requires reading instruction to be research-proven
Document 2: Revises provisions for military recruiter access to secondary school student information
requires LEAs to notify parents about student information release
Document 3: forbids an IHE from requiring a student to agree to any restriction on the student's ability to pursue a claim
amends the Higher Education Act of 1965

topic.name: Redistricting and Voting Reform
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at ensuring fair and independent redistricting processes and improving voting accessibility in the United States.

Document 1: Requires any congressional redistricting conducted by a state to be conducted in accordance with a redistricting plan developed by a nonpartisan agency
Requires the state to appoint a Temporary Redistricting Advisory Commission by February 15 of each redistricting year
Document 2: Establish the first Saturday and Sunday after the first Friday in November as the days for the election
Amends federal laws to establish the same polling place hours in the United States for both congressional and presidential elections
Document 3: Prohibits a state that has been redistricted after an apportionment from being redistricted again until after the next apportionment
Requires such redistricting to be conducted through a plan developed by the independent redistricting commission established in the state

topic.name: Federal Drug and Firearms Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the regulation of drug offenses and firearms control on federal lands and properties.

Document 1: Federal Lands Counterdrug Strategy and Enforcement Enhancement Act
penalties for the cultivation or manufacture of controlled substances on federal property
Document 2: Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms, and Explosives Reform and Firearms Modernization Act
increases penalties for possession of firearms by felons with prior convictions of a crime of violence or a serious drug offense
Document 3: prohibits the EPA from limiting firearm ammunition based on material composition

topic.name: Aviation Safety and Security Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing safety and security protocols in the aviation industry.

Document 1: requires installation of secondary barriers to prevent unauthorized access to the flight deck
cockpit doors must remain locked while in flight
Document 2: prohibits use of whole-body imaging technology as the sole method of screening
requires passengers to be informed about privacy policies related to screening technology
Document 3: prescribes rules for airport planning and development based on passenger boardings
addresses noise compatibility planning for small airports

topic.name: Food Safety and Security
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and initiatives aimed at ensuring food safety, promoting global food security, and regulating food products.

Document 1: goal of a safe and wholesome supply of meat and meat food products
ensure food safety
Document 2: deem genetically-engineered fish to be unsafe
provisions related to new animal drugs and adulterated food
Document 3: promote global food security, resilience, and nutrition
accelerate inclusive, agricultural-led economic growth

topic.name: Local Government Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislative acts related to local governance and financial management in specific U.S. territories.

Document 1: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act
provide full reimbursement for medical assistance given to a Native Hawaiian
Document 2: Requires the Governor of the Virgin Islands to appoint a Chief Financial Officer
establishes the Virgin Islands Chief Financial Officer Search Commission
Document 3: amends the Fair Minimum Wage Act of 2007
extend through 2016 the postponement of the scheduled increase in the applicable minimum wage rate

topic.name: Servicemembers' Legal Protections
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at protecting the rights and benefits of servicemembers, particularly in relation to financial and employment matters during and after military service.

Document 1: 6% interest rate limitation on debt during military service
debt entered into to consolidate or refinance student loans
Document 2: protection of servicemembers against default judgments
private right of action for a violation of the Act retroactive to December 19, 2003
Document 3: servicemember's household insurance provisions
insurers to provide servicemembers insurance choice notice

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Acts
topic.description: A collection of U.S. legislative acts addressing various domestic and foreign policies.

Document 1: Prohibits the import of products from the People's Republic of China (PRC) into the United States
Requires the President to downgrade diplomatic relations with the PRC.
Document 2: Requires the U.S. Postal Service to bargain with unions representing career Postal Service employees
Involve work that would otherwise be performed by those employees.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Defense to provide for a program under which postal benefits are provided to a member of the Armed Forces
Makes the benefits program applicable during FY2012-FY2017.

topic.name: Drug and Crime Prevention Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on addressing the environmental and social impacts of drug abuse and crime prevention through community programs and law enforcement support.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of Agriculture and the Secretary of the Interior to carry out environmental cleanup and remediation programs involving specified lands that are contaminated with hazardous substances associated with illegal methamphetamine manufacture.
Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to authorize grants to educate students on prevention and treatment of methamphetamine abuse.
Document 2: Amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 to extend the grant program for armor vests for law enforcement officers.
Document 3: Authorizes the Attorney General to designate and provide assistance to high-intensity gang activity areas in specified states and localities.
Establishes in the U.S. Marshals Service a Short-Term State Witness Protection Section to provide protection for witnesses in state and local trials involving homicide or other major violent.

topic.name: Abortion Legislation and Accountability
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures related to abortion services, healthcare obligations, and public official accountability in financial dealings.

Document 1: Amends the Peace Corps Act to subject abortion service coverage by the Peace Corps for volunteers to the same limitations that apply to Peace Corps employees regarding coverage of abortion services.
Such provision shall not be construed to limit medical evacuation coverage.
Document 2: This bill amends the federal criminal code to require any health care practitioner who is present when a child is born alive following an abortion or attempted abortion to exercise the same degree of care as reasonably provided to any other child born alive.
An individual who commits an overt act that kills a child born alive is subject to criminal prosecution for murder.
Document 3: Amends the federal criminal code to include as a fraud offense a scheme or artifice by a public official to engage in undisclosed self-dealing.
Defines 'undisclosed self-dealing' to mean performing an official act to benefit or further a financial interest of such public official.

topic.name: Public Safety Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing public safety through support for emergency response and health measures.

Document 1: Amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 to expand the definition of 'firefighter' for purposes of death benefit provisions
Applicable to deaths and injuries which occur on or after May 4, 2002.
Document 2: Amends the Public Health Service Act with respect to asthma-related grants for child health services
Requires elementary and secondary schools to permit trained personnel to administer epinephrine.

topic.name: Military Sexual Offense Prevention
topic.description: Legislation aimed at preventing and addressing sexual offenses and hazing within the military.

Document 1: Prohibits any person convicted under federal or state law of rape, sexual assault, forcible sodomy, or incest from being commissioned or enlisting in the Armed Forces.
Requires administrative separation from the Armed Forces for any member convicted of covered offenses.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the military department concerned to develop and implement a policy to prevent and respond to hazing of members.
Establishes the Military Hazing Prevention Oversight Panel to make recommendations on policies and procedures.

topic.name: District of Columbia Autonomy Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing the legislative and budgetary autonomy of the District of Columbia from federal oversight.

repeals certain requirements for annual reporting to the federal government

topic.name: U.S. Trade Law Reforms

a flight attendant or flight crewmember will be considered to meet such requirement if he or she has worked or been paid for: (1) at least 60% of the applicable total monthly guarantee
Document 2: Prohibits a covered family member who is caring for a servicemember from being denied retention in employment
Includes as a covered family member one who, while caring for the recovering servicemember, is: (1) on invitational orders

Document 3: Prohibits adoption or foster care placement service entities from using the sexual orientation, gender identity, or marital status of a prospective adoptive or foster parent

contains less than 8.5% alcohol by volume.

topic.name: Workplace Safety Legislation

topic.name: Legislative Acts for Social Welfare
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at improving social welfare through unemployment compensation, mental health evaluations, and financial literacy education.

Document 2: Amends federal law concerning federal workers' compensation to authorize the use of clinical social workers to conduct evaluations to determine work-related emotional and mental illnesses.
Document 3: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to direct the Secretary of Education to award formula matching grants to states for financial literacy education.

topic.name: U.S. Legislation on Cuba Travel

Document 1: Prohibits the President from regulating or prohibiting travel to or from Cuba by U.S. citizens or legal residents

Document 2: Prohibits that President from regulating or prohibiting travel to or from Cuba by any U.S. person
Directs the President to rescind all regulations that so regulate or prohibit such travel or transactions
Document 3: Prohibits the regulation or prohibition of travel to or from Cuba by U.S. citizens or legal residents

topic.name: Education and Immigration Policy
topic.description: Legislation addressing the intersection of educational accountability and immigration-related documentation security.

Document 1: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to require information on the annual state report cards on student achievement at each proficiency level to be disaggregated by the same major racial groups as the decennial census of the population.

Requires the proficiency information which is disaggregated by such racial groups and by ethnicity, gender, disability status, migrant status, and status as economically disadvantaged to be cross-tabulated across all of those subgroups.
Document 2: Amends the federal criminal code to revise provisions relating to the unauthorized issuance of U.S. passports to prohibit any person, during any three-year period, from: producing, issuing, or transferring 10 or more passports without lawful authority.
Imposes a fine and/or prison term of up to 20 years for violations.

topic.name: Mining Reclamation Funding

Document 2: Amends the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act of 1977, with respect to the allocation of surface mining reclamation funds

Document 3: Amends the Mineral Leasing Act, with respect to disposition of moneys received from oil, gas, and geothermal leases
